title: A Man Called Otto
logline: A curmudgeonly widower's suicide attempts are repeatedly interrupted by his new neighbors, leading him to rediscover purpose and human connection through reluctant acts of kindness.
antagonistOrConflict: Otto's grief and isolation following his wife Sonya's death
tone: Bittersweet, heartwarming with dark comedy elements

actBreaks.description: Otto's first suicide attempt is interrupted by Marisol, forcing him into reluctant engagement with his neighbors.

validation.ruling: Otto driving Marisol and the children home from the hospital after defending them represents his fundamental shift from hostile neighbor to protective family figure, marking the end of his resistance to human connection.

actBreaks.description: Otto reveals Sonya's death and his deepest pain to Marisol, marking his emotional breakthrough and acceptance of connection.

validation.ruling: The flashback revealing Sonya's death from cancer six months ago provides the crucial backstory that explains Otto's suicidal state and completes the emotional foundation for his final transformation toward choosing life over death.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated non-linear structure that weaves present-day action with memory sequences to gradually reveal Otto's backstory and the source of his pain. The memories aren't just exposition—they're emotionally triggered by present events, creating a layered narrative where past and present inform each other. The structure mirrors Otto's psychological journey from isolation to connection, with each suicide attempt serving as a structural beat that's interrupted by human need, forcing Otto deeper into community involvement despite himself.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing alternates between intimate character moments and broader community conflicts, with memory sequences providing emotional depth without slowing momentum. The script builds through three distinct suicide attempts (scenes 30, 91, 138), each interrupted by escalating community crises that require Otto's expertise, creating a rhythm of despair countered by purpose.

incitingIncident.description: Otto rushes outside to confront his new neighbors Marisol and Tommy, who are trying to park a moving truck, leading to a heated argument about permits and parking.

validation.ruling: Otto's confrontation with new neighbors Marisol and Tommy over parking violations shatters his isolated routine and forces him into ongoing conflict with people who won't simply go away, establishing the main relationship dynamic that drives the story.

midpoint.description: Otto finishes his younger self's marriage proposal to a vision of Sonya in his car, who tells him to ask properly, leading him to begin the formal question.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. CHEVY MALIBU, TRAIN STATION PARKING LOT, 2018 - DAY

validation.ruling: Position sanity: original at 76.5%. Corrected to nearest turning-point in 35-72% window.

climax.description: Marisol finds Otto dead on his bed with his shirt half-buttoned and the cat beside him, and Tommy discovers an envelope marked 'For Marisol' behind the mirror.

validation.ruling: Otto's peaceful death in his bedroom with Marisol discovering his body and final letter represents the story's emotional peak and resolution, demonstrating his completed character arc from suicidal isolation to meaningful connection, with his natural death showing he chose life over suicide.

scenes.heading: I/E. BUSY BEAVER HARDWARE STORE, 2018 - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto shops at a hardware store, buying a screw hook and rope, becoming irritated when a teenage clerk offers to help him cut the rope.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S HOUSE – BEFORE DAWN
scenes.summary: Otto follows his morning routine, waking at 5:30 am, shaving, and setting two coffee cups on the table before sitting with one.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S HOUSE/NEIGHBORHOOD - MORNING (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: Otto patrols his neighborhood, collecting flyers, checking parking permits, and sorting recycling bins while encountering his neighbor Jimmy.

scenes.heading: EXT. STIMCO STEEL AND ALUMINUM FABRICATION CORP. - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto arrives at the parking lot of Stimco Steel and Aluminum Fabrication Corp.

scenes.heading: INT. STIMCO STEEL PRODUCTION FLOOR/MAIN OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Otto enters his workplace to find surprised co-workers gathered with a retirement cake, but he remains sullen as his boss attempts to celebrate him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto's forced retirement is revealed, establishing his loss of purpose and routine

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S CHEVY MALIBU/OTTO'S STREET – DAY
scenes.summary: Otto confronts a UPS driver for using the private road without a permit, but the driver quickly leaves without listening to Otto's complaint.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S KITCHEN/LIVING ROOM/BEDROOM - DAY/MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Otto receives a telemarketer call, hangs up in frustration, then calls the phone company to disconnect his line and begins canceling other services.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sets up a chair under a ceiling-mounted screw hook, makes a noose with his rope, and begins to put it around his neck after turning away Sonya's photo.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto's first suicide attempt begins, revealing the depth of his despair

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto rushes outside to confront his new neighbors Marisol and Tommy, who are trying to park a moving truck, leading to a heated argument about permits and parking.
scenes.turningPointNote: The new neighbors' arrival interrupts Otto's suicide attempt, introducing the key relationship that will change his trajectory

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto returns home exhausted, discovers his power has been shut off, and prepares to resume his suicide attempt while tidying his living room.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S HALLWAY, FRONT DOOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marisol and Tommy arrive at Otto's door with food, attempting to introduce themselves properly as new neighbors while Otto remains hostile and dismissive.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto eats Marisol's food, feeds the leftovers to a stray cat, then returns to his noose and kicks the chair out from under himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY - SUNSET/DUSK
scenes.summary: Otto visits Sonya's grave at a cemetery, bringing flowers and talking to her about the new neighbors while sitting in a lawn chair.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Snow falls outside Otto's house at night.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto wakes early from a dream of Sonya lying beside him, reaching out to find his hand empty.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILITARY ENTRANCE STATION, 1973 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto sits on an exam table at a military entrance station as a doctor examines him with a kind smile.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM (2018) - MORNING/INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Otto lies in his bedroom, remembering the past.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN TERMINAL, 1973 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto walks through a train station, buys a ticket, and heads down to the platform while passing young men in military uniforms.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN PLATFORM, 1973 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto spots Sonya on the opposite train platform, sees her drop a book, and rushes to retrieve it as her train arrives.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, 1973 (MEMORY) - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Young Otto finds Sonya on the train, returns her book, and accepts her invitation to sit with her as they introduce themselves.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto and Sonya's first meeting establishes the central relationship of Otto's life

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM (2018) - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto wakes in bed remembering the past, discovers his alarm clock stopped when the power went out, and hurries to get dressed.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto returns Marisol's empty container with a thank-you note and notices the cat bowl he left out is now empty.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto encounters Jimmy during his neighborhood rounds, then confronts a Dye & Merica real estate agent who drives around the gate onto the private road.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto finds his neighbor Barb throwing stones at his house, trying to drive away the stray cat that scratched her dog Prince.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S FRONT DOOR/LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto patches the ceiling hole from his failed suicide attempt when Marisol and Tommy arrive with more food and ask to borrow a ladder for their jammed window.

scenes.heading: I/E. ANITA AND REUBEN’S HOUSE, FRONT DOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto visits Anita's house, offering to bleed her radiators in exchange for his borrowed hose.

scenes.heading: INT. ANITA AND REUBEN’S HOUSE, FRONT ENTRY/LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Anita tells Otto that the realty company is forcing her and her husband Reuben into care facilities, and Otto sees Reuben's deteriorated condition in his wheelchair.
scenes.turningPointNote: The threat to Otto's longtime neighbors reveals the broader forces destroying his community

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto puts on a suit and retrieves Sonya's quarter from his bedside table.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S STREET/OTTO’S GARAGE – DAY
scenes.summary: Otto walks to his garage carrying the hose and duct tape while Tommy works on the ladder in the background.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S GARAGE/CHEVY MALIBU - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Otto tapes the hose to his car's exhaust pipe, runs it through the back window, eats a cookie, starts the engine, and settles back as exhaust fills the car.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN PLATFORM, 1973 - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sits on a foggy train platform, sees a woman who looks like Sonya board a train he cannot catch, then Young Otto appears as Sonya emerges from the fog.

scenes.heading: I/E. RESTAURANT, 1973 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Young Otto waits outside a restaurant with flowers as Sonya arrives 15 minutes late by taxi, completely unconcerned about the time.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT, 1973 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Young Otto and Sonya eat dinner in awkward silence until she asks about his interests, leading him to enthusiastically explain his passion for machines and engines.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S GARAGE/CHEVY MALIBU - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol pounds on Otto's garage door, interrupting his carbon monoxide suicide attempt to tell him Tommy fell off the ladder and needs a ride to the hospital.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto's second suicide attempt is interrupted by his neighbors' crisis, forcing him back into human connection

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S CHEVY MALIBU - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto airs out his car and covers the backseat with newspaper while Marisol returns with her daughters and their car seats.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL ENTRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: The group arrives at the hospital emergency room entrance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL FAMILY WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto reluctantly reads a children's book to Abbie in the hospital waiting room, using a bear voice at her insistence while Luna plays with wrestler dolls.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL ENTRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto confronts hospital security officers outside while the children laugh about him hitting a clown.

scenes.heading: I/E. HOSPITAL FAMILY WAITING ROOM/HALLWAY (FLASHBACK) - DAY
scenes.summary: A hospital clown named Beppo performs a coin trick for the children, but Otto realizes the returned quarter isn't his 1964 silver one and demands it back from the clown's pocket.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S CHEVY MALIBU, HOSPITAL PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sits in his car with the clown's torn pocket on the dashboard as Marisol laughs, saying Otto's hidden smile reminds her of her father.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tommy, now with a broken leg and crutches, rides in Otto's backseat as they return home, with Otto refusing to drop him directly at his door.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto chases down the Dye & Merica agent's car, confronting him about driving over the curb, and learns the agent knows his reputation as someone who won't budge on development.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jimmy and Marisol bring a half-frozen stray cat into Otto's house, with Jimmy warming it inside his sweatsuit while Otto protests about the mess and chaos.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marisol admires Otto's custom-lowered kitchen counters, and Jimmy reveals that Otto's wife Sonya has passed away.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUCAS’S FARMHOUSE, DRIVEWAY 1976 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto meets Sonya's father Lucas at a rural farmhouse, where Sonya introduces him to her cat Ernest who eats from a colorful bowl.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCAS’S BARN/TRUCK (MEMORY) - DUSK
scenes.summary: Young Otto examines Lucas's truck engine in the barn, diagnosing timing issues and suggesting repairs while Sonya watches from the driveway.

scenes.heading: I/E. CHEVY MALIBU, TRAIN STATION PARKING LOT, 2018 - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sits in his car at the train station parking lot, remembering the past as he watches commuters arrive.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM/PARKING LOT, 1978 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto and Sonya celebrate among college graduates in 1978, both wearing graduation robes.

scenes.heading: I/E. CHEVY CHEVETTE, 1978 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto and Sonya sit in his car as she reads his engineering diploma aloud, but Otto interrupts with the beginning of an important question.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEVY MALIBU, TRAIN STATION PARKING LOT, 2018 - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto finishes his younger self's marriage proposal to a vision of Sonya in his car, who tells him to ask properly, leading him to begin the formal question.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto's memory of proposing to Sonya represents a crucial emotional breakthrough in his grief journey

scenes.heading: I/E. PARKING LOT/OTTO'S CHEVETTE, 1978 (MEMORY) - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Young Otto proposes to Sonya in his car as excited graduates storm past celebrating, and she accepts his proposal amid the chaos of honking horns.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto and Sonya get engaged, establishing their central relationship

scenes.heading: I/E. CHEVY MALIBU/ TRAIN STATION PARKING LOT, 2018 - DAY
scenes.summary: Present-day Otto sits alone in his car at a train station parking lot, watching a train depart before getting out and walking toward the station.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO’S TRAIN STATION, 2018 - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto positions himself at the edge of the train platform to jump in front of an approaching train, but a businessman collapses onto the tracks and Otto realizes no one else will help him.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S MALIBU, 2018/ CHEVETTE, 1980 (MEMORY) -
scenes.summary: Otto drives past chain stores and strip malls, noticing a closed Sears store that triggers a memory of the same area when it was mostly undeveloped.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO’S MALIBU/OTTO AND SONYA’S HOUSE, 1980 (MEMORY) -
scenes.summary: Otto stops in front of his future house with Sonya, who immediately loves it despite Otto's concerns about construction quality, then they move in with help from neighbors.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S HOUSE/NEIGHBORHOOD 1980 (MEMORY) - DAY/MONTAGE
scenes.summary: A montage shows Young Otto and Sonya settling into their new home with help from neighbors Young Anita and Young Reuben, building friendships through shared tasks.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S CHEVY MALIBU, 2018 - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sits in his car outside his garage when Marisol surprises him with a drawing from Abbie and asks him to be her driving instructor, while Jimmy arrives with a cat in a box.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto visits Sonya's grave, replacing old flowers with fresh ones and talking to her about his difficulty with suicide and the cat, promising to join her soon.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S BATHROOM/BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Otto finds the cat on his bed, moves it to a towel by the door, and sees Sonya's reflection in the window as he looks at Abbie's drawing.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM (2018) - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto wakes up to find the cat sleeping next to him in bed, holding the cat's tail in his palm.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S DINING ROOM/KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto feeds the cat and pours two cups of coffee, toasting before gazing out the window into a memory.

scenes.heading: I/E. LUCAS’S FARMHOUSE, 1981 (MEMORY) - EVENING
scenes.summary: At Lucas's farmhouse, a coroner and police officer speak with grieving Sonya and Young Otto while Ernest the cat checks his empty food bowl on the porch.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO AND SONYA’S HOUSE, 1981 (MEMORY) - DUSK
scenes.summary: Young Otto unloads fishing gear while Sonya sits on the porch in shadow, telling him he'll have to love her twice as much now, and Ernest the cat takes Otto's seat.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S HOUSE/STREET, 2018 – MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto confronts Malcolm about using the bike for delivering flyers, learning Malcolm works three jobs and that Sonya was his teacher who first treated him with respect as a transgender student.

scenes.heading: I/E. MARISOL & TOMMY'S FRONT PORCH/OTTO’S FORD - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto arrives at Marisol and Tommy's house wearing a wrestling mask, offering to give Marisol driving lessons after criticizing the current arrangement.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S CHEVY MALIBU/LOCAL STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto teaches Marisol to drive his manual transmission car, yanking the parking brake when she nearly hits another vehicle at a red light.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S CHEVY MALIBU/STREET OUTSIDE A PASTRY SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol successfully parallel parks Otto's car under his patient instruction, both feeling accomplished by the achievement.

scenes.heading: INT. PASTRY SHOP AND CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto and Marisol sit in a pastry shop eating Swedish semlor, with Otto explaining how he and Sonya used to come here every Saturday at one o'clock.

scenes.heading: EXT. A BIRCHWOOD FOREST - DAY
scenes.summary: A worker cuts down birch trees in a forest that has been mostly cleared for condo development, as Otto narrates how the neighborhood lost its namesake trees.

scenes.heading: INT. PASTRY SHOP AND CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto continues telling Marisol about his conflict with Reuben over car preferences, showing a montage of their competing Ford and Chevy purchases over the years.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto tells Marisol about Reuben's estranged relationship with his son Chris, who moved to Japan and never visits.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S STREET/REUBEN’S GARAGE, 1995 (MEMORY) – DAY
scenes.summary: Otto approaches Reuben with expensive whisky to make peace, and Reuben accepts, saying they've always been friends and offering to show Otto his new car.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFÉ, 2018 – DAY
scenes.summary: Otto tells Marisol that Reuben's new car was an automatic, which Otto saw as a betrayal of principles, and when Marisol asks about children, Otto abruptly ends the conversation.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S GARAGE – DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol asks Otto to babysit so she and Tommy can go to dinner, clarifying they'll take a cab and just need him to watch the children.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE – EVENING
scenes.summary: Tommy shows Otto the alarm system while Marisol gets ready, with Otto expressing doubts about babysitting as they prepare to leave.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY’S LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Otto babysits Luna and Abbie, learning Spanish words from a wrestling book and playing with wrestling dolls, calling himself 'Abuelo Luchador.'

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY’S KITCHEN – EVENING
scenes.summary: Otto helps Luna make chocolate milk for sleepy Abbie in the kitchen, noticing an uninstalled dishwasher that Luna calls 'goddamn useless.'

scenes.heading: I/E. LUNA AND ABBIE'S BEDROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Otto watches Luna design houses on her computer while Abbie drinks chocolate milk, impressed by Luna's architectural skills.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE, FRONT HALL – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marisol and Tommy return home exhausted but happy, finding the house quiet as they head upstairs to check on the children.

scenes.heading: INT. LUNA AND ABBIE'S BEDROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marisol and Tommy find Otto and Luna still at the computer with sleeping Abbie in Otto's lap, and Luna gives Otto a toy hand to pat himself on the back for doing well.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY’S HOUSE, FRONT HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tommy thanks Otto and says goodnight from the front door, then sets the alarm system.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY’S KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tommy discovers that Otto has secretly installed their dishwasher while babysitting, surprising both him and Marisol.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Otto lies in bed staring at the ceiling, then reaches out to pet the cat on the other pillow, which purrs contentedly.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto discovers groundskeepers installing artificial turf where the real estate agent previously drove on the grass near the neighborhood gate.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO’S NEIGHBORHOOD, RECYCLING AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto mutters angrily about corporate changes while sorting recycling, then stops Malcolm to point out his bike's chain problem and offers to help fix it.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO’S STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Social media journalist Shari Kenzie asks Jimmy for directions to find Otto Andersen, and Jimmy points her toward Otto's garage.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto helps Malcolm fix his bike's derailleur and gives him tools, learning Malcolm is saving for a Volkswagen, when journalist Shari Kenzie approaches Otto for an interview about his heroic act.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S FRONT DOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol knocks on Otto's door after the journalist leaves, and they walk together enjoying a moment of connection as she offers to do something for him in return for his help.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto and Marisol's friendship deepens as she offers to reciprocate his kindness

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S HOUSE, HALLWAY/FRONT DOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto storms into his house in rage, slamming and locking the door while Marisol pounds outside demanding he talk to her, and Otto buries his face in hanging coats.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sits on his couch ignoring Marisol's pleas through the window until she gives up and leaves, then he flashes on a memory of building a small headboard.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S ATTIC - DAY (NIAGARA MEMORIES SEQUENCE)
scenes.summary: Otto climbs into his attic and finds a covered baby crib with scrollwork that he built, remembering setting it up for pregnant Sonya, then finds a camping lantern and tattered Niagara Falls cap.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto confronts the painful memory of his lost child, triggering his final suicide attempt

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S DINING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto moves furniture and seals off his dining room with plastic sheeting and drop cloths, preparing the space for something.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Otto puts on a dress shirt then changes his mind and hangs up his good clothes, setting up an emergency radio with classical music for the cat.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S DINING ROOM - DUSK
scenes.summary: Otto sits in his underwear in the sealed dining room at dusk with a shotgun case and lantern, remembering as classical music plays upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. BUS (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: In memory, pregnant Sonya puts Young Otto's hand on her belly to feel the baby moving, and he smiles before excusing himself to go to the bus restroom.

scenes.heading: I/E. BUS/BUS BATHROOM (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto wipes away tears of happiness in the bus bathroom, but the memory shifts to present-day Otto reliving the moment when the bus crashes, throwing him violently as debris flies.
scenes.turningPointNote: The bus crash memory is fully revealed, showing the traumatic event that killed their unborn child

scenes.heading: EXT. CRASH SITE (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto climbs from the bus wreckage bruised and dazed, seeing injured passengers everywhere, and finds his camera and the torn Niagara Falls cap among the debris.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S DINING ROOM (MODERN DAY) - DUSK/INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Present-day Otto opens the shotgun case and takes out the shotgun.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL, HALLWAY (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the hospital, Dr. Knight tells Young Otto that Sonya has a serious spinal injury and their baby didn't survive the crash, while social worker Anna tries to comfort him.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S LIVING ROOM (MODERN DAY) - DUSK/INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Present-day Otto loads the shotgun in his living room.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM/BATHROOM (MEMORY) - DAY/EVENING
scenes.summary: Young Otto sits by unconscious Sonya's hospital bedside over multiple days, and when she finally extends her finger while still unconscious, he takes it in his palm before she opens her eyes.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sonya awakens from her coma, marking her survival and recovery beginning

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S DINING ROOM (MODERN DAY) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Otto positions a shotgun barrel in his mouth and puts his finger on the trigger, but stops when he remembers Sonya in her wheelchair telling him he's angry and sad.

scenes.heading: I/E. OTTO'S HALLWAY, FRONT DOOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Otto opens his door in underwear holding a shotgun, startling Malcolm who asks to crash on Otto's couch for the night after being thrown out.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S FRONT HALL/ STAIRS/ UPSTAIRS HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Otto leads Malcolm upstairs to sleep on a couch in Sonya's study, telling him his father is an idiot for not accepting him as Malcolm.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Otto returns to the dining room and cleans up plaster debris that has fallen from the hole in the ceiling.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO’S HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Otto's house in the morning.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto's eyes open as he wakes up in the morning.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S HALLWAY/KITCHEN/DINING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto comes downstairs to find Malcolm already feeding the cat and cooking eggs, and Malcolm offers him coffee before his morning rounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S STREET/GATE/RECYCLING AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto, Malcolm, and Jimmy walk the neighborhood doing rounds together, sorting recycling bins and checking parking permits, when Jimmy mentions that Anita and Reuben are being moved to assisted living.

scenes.heading: I/E. ANITA AND REUBEN’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto demands to see all of Anita's documents from Dye & Merica and tells Reuben he's been an idiot but they haven't been gotten rid of yet.

scenes.heading: I/E. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE, FRONT DOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol initially refuses to let Otto use her phone because he won't explain why his is disconnected, but eventually agrees after he storms off.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE, DINING ROOM/KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto tells Marisol about the bus crash that killed their unborn baby and left Sonya paralyzed, explaining how they fought for wheelchair accessibility in their community.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto finally opens up about his tragic past, revealing the source of his grief and anger

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO AND SONYA’S HOUSE, 1981 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto wheels Sonya in her wheelchair up a ramp to their house, noticing new construction that lacks wheelchair accessibility.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNITY CENTER, 1982 (MEMORY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Otto carries Sonya into a community center meeting where he confronts Dye & Merica representatives about accessibility issues.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto tells Marisol he was voted out as head of the Homeowner's Association and that Sonya helped him keep living instead of seeking revenge.

scenes.heading: INT. ANOTHER HOSPITAL ROOM, SIX MONTHS AGO - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sits beside Sonya's hospital bed holding her hand as she dies from cancer six months ago.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY’S HOUSE, DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Otto tells Marisol he was going to join Sonya in death but thinks she wants him to keep living, then prepares to make a phone call.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto decides to live and take action rather than commit suicide

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO'S STREET, GATE ENTRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: A Dye & Merica agent opens the gate to let a wheelchair transport van drive through to collect Reuben.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANITA AND REUBEN'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto sits calmly with the cat as the Dye & Merica agent and transport workers arrive, while Anita refuses to let them take Reuben and a news crew films the confrontation that ultimately forces the company to back down.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto successfully orchestrates the defeat of Dye & Merica's attempt to take Reuben

scenes.heading: INT. ANITA AND REUBEN'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto tells Reuben that the corporate representatives gave up without a fight and that it feels good they haven't been gotten rid of yet.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANITA AND REUBEN’S HOUSE/STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto tries to slip away from the news interviews but staggers and collapses on the pavement, prompting Marisol to rush to his side and call for an ambulance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: A nurse tells Marisol she can now visit Otto in the hospital.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol visits Otto in his hospital room where Dr. Ellis explains he has an enlarged heart condition that could be fatal at any time.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Blue balloons fly outside Marisol and Tommy's house.

scenes.heading: INT. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto arrives at a baby celebration party where Marisol introduces him to guests as her friend, and he appears uncomfortable with the attention but stays.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto introduces Marisol's family to Sonya's headstone at the cemetery, with the children placing flowers and Marisol waving the baby's hand.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto has trouble buttoning his shirt while getting dressed, indicating his declining health.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto eats breakfast and feeds the cat, experiencing chest pain that forces him to sit down before it subsides.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Otto writes a letter at Sonya's vanity and places it with documents in an envelope behind the mirror.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tommy carries a box from Otto's house and places it in Otto's car trunk.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO’S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM/ENTRY HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto paints over a repaired ceiling hole while Marisol packs up Sonya's coats, and Otto nods his approval for her to continue.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTTO’S GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto teaches Malcolm and Jimmy how to change the car's oil, then gives Malcolm the car registration, keys, and ownership of his Chevy Malibu.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto arrives in a new Chevy Silverado and proudly shows it off to Marisol's family, explaining he wanted room for everyone.

scenes.heading: I/E. CHEVY SILVERADO - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto installs a booster seat and drives off with Marisol and the girls in his new truck, saying 'This is living.'

scenes.heading: I/E. CHEVY SILVERADO/OPEN ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Silverado drives down a winding road as time passes and winter returns.

scenes.heading: I/E. MARISOL AND TOMMY'S FRONT PORCH/KITCHEN/HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: A UPS package is delivered to Marisol's house where the family prepares for Marco's third birthday party, and family photos on the wall show Otto integrated into their lives.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S FRONT DOOR, HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol rushes into Otto's house calling his name with mounting anguish, followed by Tommy.

scenes.heading: INT. OTTO'S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol finds Otto dead on his bed with his shirt half-buttoned and the cat beside him, and Tommy discovers an envelope marked 'For Marisol' behind the mirror.
scenes.turningPointNote: Otto dies peacefully of natural causes, having found purpose and family

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH FUNERAL SERVICE - DAY
scenes.summary: A minister speaks at Otto's funeral while Otto's voiceover requests a simple remembrance, and mourners including all his neighbors and friends fill the church.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHURCH RECEPTION HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Shari Kenzie reports on Otto as a local hero outside the church reception hall while Malcolm films the segment.

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH RECEPTION HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mourners gather at the reception with food and a cake featuring Otto's photo while Otto's voiceover explains his financial arrangements for Marisol.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHURCH RECEPTION HALL/PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Marisol's family leaves the reception and goes to Otto's Chevy, where Marisol takes the keys from Tommy following Otto's posthumous instructions not to let him drive.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto's name appears carved beside Sonya's on their shared headstone with pink flowers, as Otto's voiceover says 'Abuelo Otto' before the film ends.

structuralWarnings.text: Act 3 is only 12% of the script — structural placement suspect
